revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Module:Ordinal]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Module:Ordinal]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: hopefully last batch of imports
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Module:Ordinal]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Module:Ordinal]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category New automatic wikipedia redirects
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Module:Ordinal]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Module:Ordinal]]</includeonly> <noinclude></noinclude>

revision.comment: [[Module:Ordinal/testcases]] doesn't exist; no prejudice against reverting when the testcases are created
revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Manual reimport from enwiki to fix erroneous transclusions
revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

"</sup>" end return n .. suffix end

--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.

Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end

--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end

"</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]

Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]

Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]

local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation

Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Manual reimport from enwiki to fix erroneous transclusions
revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.

Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.

--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.

Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end

--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}

Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end

"</sup>" end return n .. suffix end

revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

revision.comment: [[Module:Ordinal/testcases]] doesn't exist; no prejudice against reverting when the testcases are created
revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Manual reimport from enwiki to fix erroneous transclusions
revision.text:
--[[
This template will add the appropriate ordinal suffix to a given integer.
Please do not modify this code without applying the changes first at Module:Ordinal/sandbox and testing.
]]
local p = {}
local yesno = require('Module:Yesno') -- boolean value interpretation
--[[ This function converts an integer value into a numeral followed by ordinal indicator. The output string might contain HTML tags.
Usage: {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal|1=|2=|sup=}} {{#invoke:Ordinal|ordinal}} - uses the caller's parameters
Parameters 1: Any number or string. 2: Set to "d" if the module should display "d" instead of "nd" and "rd". sup: Set to yes/no to toggle superscript ordinal suffix. ]] function p.ordinal(frame) local args = frame.args if args[1] == nil then args = frame:getParent().args end if args[1] == nil then args[1] = "{{{1}}}" end return p._ordinal(args[1], (args[2] == 'd'), yesno(args.sup)) end
function p._ordinal(n, d, sup) local x = tonumber(mw.ustring.match(n, "(%d*)%W*$")) local suffix = "th" -- If tonumber(n) worked: if x then local mod10 = math.abs(x) % 10 local mod100 = math.abs(x) % 100 if mod10 == 1 and mod100 ~= 11 then suffix = "st" elseif mod10 == 2 and mod100 ~= 12 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "nd" end elseif mod10 == 3 and mod100 ~= 13 then if d then suffix = "d" else suffix = "rd" end end end if sup then suffix = "<sup>" .. suffix .. "</sup>" end return n .. suffix end
return p
